title: Orlok

{{Quote|The Empire ''stuck'' me on this storm-swept excuse for a world…I'm going to ''prove'' I'm worthy of far, ''far'' more than running a training station!|Commander Orlok|<ref name="Trap" />}}
'''Orlok''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] serving with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the leading Imperial authority on ground-based facilities during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], shortly following the Imperial defeat at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Orlok was assigned to command the [[Imperial Training Center]] on the remote, storm-ridden [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Daluuj/Legends|Daluuj]]. Orlok resented the position, believing himself to be worth more to the Empire than running a mere training station on such a backwater world.

The opportunity for Orlok to prove his greater value to the Empire came six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], when several jettisoned [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] delivered the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] and several Calamari agents to Daluuj following an Imperial attack while they were attempting to rendezvous with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Orlok set out from the training fort with a convoy of three [[Imperial ground-skimmer]]s carrying [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to respond to the Rebel crash site, but the commander quickly found himself in a race with the Rebels [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and the [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Heroes of Yavin]], traveling in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], to reach Ackbar's position.

While the Rebels linked up with Ackbar first, they were beset by two giant [[lake worm]]s that dragged the ''Millennium Falcon'' to the [[Water/Legends|watery]] depths of a nearby [[mud lake]]. Solo and Skywalker set out to stall Orlok's impending arrival, while Ackbar's team worked to free the Rebel [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. In the resulting [[Battle of Daluuj|skirmish]], Orlok succeeded in driving Solo and Skywalker back to the Rebel crash site, but he met his defeat when Ackbar strategically drew Orlok's ground-skimmers to the center of the lake, where the giant [[Worm/Legends|worms]] dragged Orlok and his men to the water's bottom, simultaneously releasing the ''Millennium Falcon''.

==Biography==
===Detested assignment on Daluuj===
{{Quote|This is my opportunity to ''show'' the Empire I'm worthy of ''more'' than this backwater duty station! I mean to ''use'' it!|Commander Orlok|<ref name="Trap" />}}
Orlok was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] during the prime [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> Orlok was second only to [[Bevel Lemelisk]],<ref name="Rebellion Guide">[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Rebellion'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> [[Chief engineer/Legends|Chief Engineer]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]],<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> in terms of being the top authority on Imperial facilities. When it came to ground-based facilities, however, Orlok was the best the Empire had to offer.<ref name="Rebellion Guide" />

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Empire had assigned [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Orlok<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> to be in charge of the [[Imperial Training Center]]<ref name="SWE" /> on the [[Water/Legends|rainswept]], backwater<ref name="Trap" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Daluuj/Legends|Daluuj]], much to Orlok's displeasure.<ref name="CSWE-Orlok">{{CSWECite|II|424|Orlok, Commander}}</ref> He<ref name="SWE" /> resented being posted on the stormy world to command such an insignificant outpost.<ref name="CSWE-Orlok" /> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]], six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the Imperial defeat at [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]],<ref name="Date">The [[Battle of Daluuj]] immediately precedes the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Evacuation of Yavin]] in the ''[[Classic Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the Evacuation of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] to six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], thereby providing an approximate date for the Battle of [[Daluuj/Legends|Daluuj]].</ref> several [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] carrying the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] and some of his Calamari colleagues crash-landed on Daluuj. Ackbar and his men had fallen under Imperial attack while attempting to rendezvous with [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] to join the Rebellion's [[Evacuation of Yavin|evacuation]] efforts of its [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] and were forced to jettison to Daluuj's surface. The Imperial Training Center quickly detected the signal emanating from Ackbar's [[Distress beacon/Legends|distress beacon]], and Orlok set out to investigate, leading a force of three [[Imperial ground-skimmer]]s loaded with [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Orlok hoped this [[Battle of Daluuj|opportunity]] would be his chance to prove his greater value to the Empire and would be his ticket off Daluuj.<ref name="Trap" />

===Orlok's opportunity===
{{Quote|If by some miracle they survive to ''land'', I mean to see they ''never'' take off! This venture will be my ticket ''away'' from Daluuj!|Commander Orlok|<ref name="Trap" />}}
[[File:Orlok-CSW.png|thumb|left|240px|Orlok was determined to prove he was worthy of more than the remote Daluuj training station.]]
While approaching Ackbar's position, however, the [[Unidentified Imperial Army pilot (Orlok)|pilot]] of Orlok's ground-skimmer informed the commander that their instruments indicated a [[Starship/Legends|ship]] passing them overhead. Although Orlok derided as mad anyone attempting to fly through Daluuj's constant [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] storms, he nevertheless knew they were now in a race for the source of the distress beacon. What Orlok did not know was that the competing ship was none other than the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], carrying the Rebels [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], Leia Organa, and the rest of the [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Heroes of Yavin]], en route to rescue Ackbar and his men. Orlok ordered his three ground-skimmers ahead at full speed, intent on preventing the Rebels from ever leaving Daluuj.<ref name="Trap" />

The Rebels reached Ackbar's crash site ahead of Orlok, though Solo was forced to set the ''Millennium Falcon'' down on the surface of a [[mud lake]], the only available landing spot. Before they could rescue the downed Mon Calamari, two giant [[lake worm]]s emerged from the water and dragged the ''Millennium Falcon'' below in their grasp. The Rebels were able to swim to freedom on the shores of the lake but with the realization that they were now trapped on Daluuj without a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] and with Orlok's forces inexorably bearing down on them.<ref name="Trap" />

Orlok's ground-skimmers soon hit a patch of dense fog, forcing the lead [[Scout/Legends|scout]] craft to stop in its approach. Angered, Orlok demanded to know why their progress had been halted. The lead craft's [[Unidentified Imperial Army pilot (lead ground-skimmer)|pilot]] reported echoes and distortions on their [[Scanner|scanning]] equipment because of the fog and warned that proceeding further would only risk wrecking the vehicles or missing the Rebels' position. Not to be denied his opportunity, Orlok ordered the stalled craft to hold its position—he would be taking the lead himself.<ref name="Trap" />

However, Solo, Skywalker, and the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]—having set out from the Rebels' crash site together to slow the Imperials while Ackbar and his men worked to free the ''Millennium Falcon'' from the lake—ambushed the waiting lead ground-skimmer before Orlok could reach its position, neutralizing the craft's stormtroopers, but the commotion they made in the attack alerted Orlok and the main Imperial party. When the fog cleared away soon thereafter, opening the way for the rest of the Imperials to rush to the aid of their attacked comrades, Orlok devised a plan to instead gain the surprise advantage on the Rebels. He split his forces in two, sending the remaining two ground-skimmers forward and using the sound of their approaching [[Engine/Legends|engines]] to mask a large group of stormtroopers, led by Orlok, secretly taking up positions atop a nearby bluff.<ref name="Trap" />

===A watery disposition===
{{Quote|Back to your ''vehicles'', they can't be ''far!'' Now we'll end this game…''and'' every last one of our elusive ''opponents'' as well!|Commander Orlok|<ref name="Trap" />}}
[[File:OrlokWorms-CSW.png|thumb|right|300px|Daluuj's giant lake worms drag Orlok's ground-skimmers to the bottom of mud lake.]]
At Orlok's command, the stormtroopers atop the bluff began firing down at will on the Rebels, who scrambled into the Imperials' neutralized ground-skimmer and started speeding away. Solo, at the ground-skimmer's controls, expertly avoided the Imperial fire, but Orlok ordered his men to keep at it, as the Rebels were heading straight into range of the guns of Orlok's two other approaching vehicles. However, Solo, not planning on Orlok's ambush, had previously cross-wired the skimmer to overload once the Imperials eventually recovered it, forcing he, Skywalker, and Chewbacca to leap from the vehicle just moments before its destruction. The explosion covered their escape from the Imperials, but it did not stop their approach. Orlok and his men soon inspected the exploded skimmer and determined that the Rebels had, in fact, escaped for the moment. Determined to defeat the elusive Rebels once and for all, Orlok ordered his stormtroopers back to the remaining ground-skimmers to continue pursuit.<ref name="Trap" />

Orlok's two ground-skimmers reached the mud lake's edge and opened fire as they approached the Rebel crash site. Ackbar ordered his group to take up a vulnerable position across the lake from the Imperials but nevertheless in such a spot so as to draw Orlok's skimmers across the center of the lake in order to reach them. The stratagem worked just as Ackbar planned—as Orlok reached the lake's center, the two giant worms emerged from the water and coiled themselves around the skimmers, dragging them down to the bottom of the lake and releasing the ''Millennium Falcon'' in the process. With Orlok defeated and their ship freed, the Rebels safely escaped Daluuj with Ackbar and his fellow Mon Calamari.<ref name="Trap" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Rebels aren't the ''only'' ones capable of surprises! Men…''fire at will!''|Commander Orlok|<ref name="Trap" />}}
[[File:OrlokAttacks-CSW.png|thumb|left|300px|Orlok orders his stormtroopers to fire down on the Rebels from atop the bluff.]]
Orlok was an ambitious Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officer]] and one who resented his command at the remote Imperial training outpost on<ref name="CSWE-Orlok" /> wet, fog-shrouded Daluuj.<ref name="CSWE-Daluuj">{{CSWECite|I|162|Daluuj}}</ref> He felt he was worth more to the Empire than running a minor training station and recognized the Rebel presence on Daluuj as his opportunity to rise to greater things. Orlok vowed to [[Death/Legends|eliminate]] the Rebels to prevent them from ever accomplishing their mission of rescuing Ackbar's downed team.<ref name="Trap" />

During the skirmish with the Rebels, Orlok demonstrated a certain ability for tactical innovation. Once Han Solo and Luke Skywalker defeated the stormtroopers in Orlok's lead ground-skimmer, Orlok, realizing that his enemy expected him to rush to the aid of the lost skimmer, instead elected to divide his force. He personally led a stormtrooper party on a disguised flanking maneuver, which succeeded in catching the Rebels by surprise and forcing them on the run directly into firing range of his other two waiting skimmers. Only Solo's inadvertently-manufactured cover explosion allowed the Rebels to escape Orlok's trap. Nevertheless, Orlok was later bested by Ackbar, himself regarded as a tactical genius in his own right.<ref name="Trap" />

Orlok was a demanding combat leader during the battle, constantly urging his ground-skimmers to move faster in the race to reach the source of Ackbar's distress beacon. He scolded his lead skimmer for daring to halt without his permission when it ran into a dense fog cover and stopped rather than risking damage or bypassing the downed Rebels' position. Instead, Orlok dismissed the threat and took lead of the convoy himself.<ref name="Trap" />

Orlok was the Empire's foremost authority on ground-based facilities, second only to Chief [[Engineer/Legends|Engineer]] Bevel Lemelisk as top overall Imperial facility specialist.<ref name="Rebellion Guide" /> He had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]],<ref name="Trap" /> blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and light skin. Orlok wore a goatee around the time of the Battle of Yavin.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" />

==Equipment==
Commander Orlok wore the standard-issue [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|Imperial officer's uniform]] during the Battle of Daluuj. He also utilized a pair of [[Binoculars/Legends|binoculars]] while directing his stormtroopers to attack the Rebels from atop a bluff.<ref name="Trap" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ImpPilotOrlok-CSW.png|thumb|right|220px|''It's a Trap'' incorrectly illustrates Commander Orlok with an Imperial admiral's rank badge.]]
Commander Orlok first appeared in "[[It's a Trap]]", a comic strip written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by artist [[Al Williamson]]. "It's a Trap" was originally published by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] as a newspaper [[Star Wars (comic strip)|serial comic]] across [[November]] [[1982]] to [[January]] [[1983]], leading up to the [[May]] 1983 theatrical release of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="Trap" />

Orlok was later included in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] as a playable character available to the Galactic Empire.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> The game established Orlok as the Empire's leading ground-facility expert. In gameplay, the character has the invaluable ability to research new facility designs and also possesses leadership and combat ratings that enable him to successfully quell planetary uprisings and conduct missions to Alliance systems. The player may also grant Orlok the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[General/Legends|general]] for advanced ability in commanding ground [[Troop/Legends|troops]].<ref name="Rebellion Guide" />

===Inconsistencies===
The ''[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' publications each misspell the character's name as "'''Orlock'''."<ref name="Rebellion Guide" /><ref name="Comics Companion">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref>

In one panel of the comic strip, Orlok's [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank insignia plaque]] includes six red bars,<ref name="Trap" /> which corresponds to the Imperial Military rank of admiral, according to the [[1994]] [[West End Games]] [[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''Imperial Sourcebook'']].<ref name="Imperial Sourcebook">[[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''Imperial Sourcebook'', Second Edition]]</ref> However, in a later scene, Orlok's rank badge includes three red bars,<ref name="Trap" /> which, according to the ''Imperial Sourcebook'', correctly corresponds to his rank of commander.<ref name="Imperial Sourcebook" /> Orlok's profile picture from ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' depicts the character with both the correct rank badge and number of [[Imperial code cylinder|code cylinders]] in accordance with his commander rank.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" />
